title: Joe Corroney

{{Quote|Most of us artists out there who have been doing Star Wars art for years already had our bodies surgically altered with the 'Lucasfilm implant.' Right after I signed the contract for my very first ''Star Wars'' project about twelve years ago all I can remember are a couple of guys in stormtrooper armor showing up at my apartment and abducting me in the middle of the night and then waking up at Skywalker Ranch the next day wandering around Lake Ewok in my pajamas with some fresh stitches in the back of my head. The implant keeps us from talking about scripts, sharing photographs and exposing other top secret information about their movies before they are released for fear of our heads exploding.|Joe Corroney|<ref name="Raider">{{WebCite|url=http://www.theraider.net/features/interviews/joe_corroney.php|text=Joe Corroney Interview|author=Mitchell Hallock|date=2008-05-05|archivedate=20191228035225}}</ref>}}
'''Joe Corroney''' is an illustrator who has provided [[Lucasfilm]] with official ''[[Star Wars]]'' artwork for related books, games, trading cards and magazines since [[1996]]. He also penciled ''[[Star Wars: Empire]]'' for [[Dark Horse Comics]].

Other [[Star Wars comic books|comic books]] he has illustrated include ''Crimson Dynamo'' for Marvel Comics, ''Green Lantern'' for DC Comics, ''Parts Unknown'' for Image, and ''Blood and Roses'' for Blue Line Pro. Along with developing his creator-owned comic-book series, ''Death Avenger'', Corroney recently illustrated ''Spike Vs. Dracula'' for [[IDW Publishing]]. Currently, he is creating new ''Star Wars'' artwork for Lucasfilm and ''[[Star Wars Insider]]''.

Corroney is the creator of the ''Star Wars'' characters [[Kai Justiss]] and [[Ur-Sema Du]], who are based on his fellow artists [[Justin Chung]] and [[Jan Duursema]], respectively.

Corroney later illustrated ''[[Star Wars Propaganda: A History of Persuasive Art in the Galaxy]]'' and ''[[Star Wars: The Rebel Files]]'',<ref name="Joe Corroney">{{SW|url=news/nycc-2017-artist-joe-corroney-on-the-upcoming-star-wars-rebel-files-book|text=NYCC 2017: Artist Joe Corroney On The Upcoming ''Star Wars: Rebel Files'' Book}}</ref> released in [[2016]]<ref name="Propaganda">''[[Star Wars Propaganda: A History of Persuasive Art in the Galaxy]]''</ref> and [[2017]] respectively.<ref name="Rebel Files">''[[Star Wars: The Rebel Files]]''</ref>

In [[2019]], 3D artist [[Ansel Hsiao]], known for his work in ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', complained that some of the artworks published on his [http://fractalsponge.net website] were reused without his consent by Joe Corroney in the [[2014]] reference book ''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]''.<ref>{{WebCite|url=http://http://fractalsponge.net/?p=4224|text=Hey, Disney and Joe Corroney|author=[[Ansel Hsiao|Hsiao, Ansel]]|date=2019-02-16|archivedate=20190224032232}}</ref><ref>{{ArtStation|username=fractalsponge|url=fractalsponge/blog/wAWe/turns-out-desaturate-in-photoshop-is-great|name=[[Ansel Hsiao]]|text=Turns out, desaturate in Photoshop is great.|archivedate=20201111204527}}</ref>
